chemical_identifiers.notes: Unimolecular GLP-1 and amylin receptor co-agonist; single-chain polypeptide

patents.title: Co-agonists of the GLP-1 and amylin receptors

pubmed_papers.title: Amycretin, a novel, unimolecular GLP-1 and amylin receptor agonist administered subcutaneously: results from a phase 1b/2a randomised controlled study

Safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of the first-in-class GLP-1 and amylin receptor agonist, amycretin: a first-in-human, phase 1, double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled trial
Amycretin in obesity: Mechanisms, clinical efficacy, and future perspectives

compounds.key_findings: Amycretin (NN9487) is a first-in-class unimolecular GLP-1/amylin receptor co-agonist developed by Novo Nordisk. Phase 1b/2a subcutaneous trial demonstrated exceptional weight loss efficacy: 24.3% at 60mg once-weekly dose (36 weeks), exceeding semaglutide (~15-17%) and approaching tirzepatide (~22.5%). Oral formulation showed up to 13.1% weight loss at 12 weeks. Safety profile acceptable: all adverse events mild-moderate, no deaths, GI events most common (49%) but consistent with GLP-1/amylin class. Two Phase 1 trials completed with results published in Lancet (July 2025). Patent WO2022129526A1 covers co-agonist compounds. Both oral (once-daily) and subcutaneous (once-weekly) formulations in development.
compounds.notes: Data extracted from comprehensive web search conducted 2026-03-20. Sources include: 2 ClinicalTrials.gov trials, 13+ PubMed papers (2 Lancet publications), 1 WIPO patent.

compounds.company: Shenzhen Salubris Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. (信立泰)
compounds.stage: Early clinical (Phase I; reported highest global phase up to Phase II in industry reports)

chemical_identifiers.notes: SAL0112 is a small‑molecule, orally active GLP‑1 receptor biased agonist developed by Shenzhen Salubris. Publicly available sources do not disclose a definitive chemical structure, SMILES, InChIKey, IUPAC name, or molecular weight. A secondary description notes that SAL‑0112 adds a benzyl methyl substituent on the aromatic ring to modify lipophilicity and pharmacokinetics, but the full structure is not reported.

pubmed_papers.title: Pharmacodynamic and pharmacokinetic profiles of a novel GLP-1 receptor biased agonist SAL0112
Toxicology profile of a novel GLP-1 receptor biased agonist SAL0112 in nonhuman primates

suppliers.role: Originator / developer; only clearly identified organization associated with supplying or developing SAL0112

compounds.key_findings:
SAL0112 is a novel, oral, small‑molecule GLP‑1 receptor biased agonist developed by Shenzhen Salubris for type 2 diabetes and obesity.

Preclinical pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics (rodent models):
- SAL0112 shows potent and selective GLP‑1 receptor biased agonism and effectively alleviates signs of type 2 diabetes in mouse models, with efficacy comparable to the injectable GLP‑1 analog liraglutide and to the small‑molecule GLP‑1 agonist danuglipron in rodent type 2 diabetes and obesity models.
- It offers a more favorable pharmacokinetic profile than danuglipron in preclinical studies.

Nonclinical safety and toxicology (cynomolgus monkey studies):
- SAL0112 exhibits high potency at the monkey GLP‑1 receptor and minimal activation of rodent GLP‑1 receptors, indicating species‑selective pharmacology.
- In hERG assays, SAL0112 has an IC50 >100 μM compared with danuglipron's ≈6.9 μM, suggesting lower potential for hERG‑mediated cardiotoxicity.
- Cardiovascular safety pharmacology in cynomolgus monkeys (single doses up to 250 mg/kg) showed no significant changes in ECG, heart rate, or blood pressure versus vehicle controls.
- In a 13‑week repeat‑dose toxicity study in cynomolgus monkeys, SAL0112 produced moderate anorexigenic effects and reduced body weight; these effects were reversible and not associated with pathological organ changes.
- No liver toxicity was observed and overall tolerance was good during repeated dosing.
- The NOAEL in cynomolgus monkeys was 150 mg/kg, corresponding to an approximate 3‑fold safety margin.

Development status:
- Industry and clinical‑trials databases identify SAL0112 as a Salubris pipeline asset for type 2 diabetes and overweight/obesity, administered orally.
- At least one first‑in‑human Phase I study in healthy volunteers and type 2 diabetes patients is registered/planned in China (Beijing Hospital), and industry reports describe SAL0112 as reaching up to Phase II globally for metabolic indications.

Overall, available data support SAL0112 as a promising oral small‑molecule GLP‑1 receptor biased agonist with:
- Preclinical efficacy in rodent models of type 2 diabetes and obesity comparable to existing GLP‑1 therapies.
- A favorable nonclinical safety profile in nonhuman primates, including a wide therapeutic window and low hERG inhibition risk.
- Advancement into early clinical development in China for type 2 diabetes and obesity.
compounds.data_quality: Moderate. Preclinical pharmacology and toxicology are described in peer‑reviewed journals with clear study designs and quantitative findings. Multiple independent industry and pipeline reports consistently list SAL0112 as a GLP‑1 receptor biased agonist in early clinical development for metabolic indications. However, critical chemistry data (exact structure, SMILES, InChIKey, molecular weight) and detailed clinical outcomes are not yet publicly disclosed, and specific composition‑of‑matter patent numbers for SAL0112 could not be confirmed from open text sources.
compounds.notes:
1) All chemical‑identifier fields (PubChem CID, SMILES, InChIKey, IUPAC name, molecular weight) remain blank because no reliable open source currently provides this information for SAL0112; any attempt to infer structure would be speculative.
2) There is some discrepancy between sources on development stage: a GlobalData listing describes a planned Phase I first‑in‑human study in China, while an industry market report and pipeline overviews describe SAL0112 as having reached Phase II in China for type 2 diabetes. This likely reflects recent or region‑specific trial progress.
3) The compound is consistently described as an oral, small‑molecule GLP‑1 receptor biased agonist intended for type 2 diabetes and obesity/overweight, with Shenzhen Salubris as originator.
4) Combination‑therapy patents and reviews list SAL0112 alongside other GLP‑1 receptor agonists as a candidate agent but do not provide additional structural details.

compounds.company: BrightGene Bio-Medical Technology Co., Ltd. (博瑞生物)

chemical_identifiers.notes: GLP-1/GIP dual agonist peptide

pubmed_papers.title: The safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of an optimized dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist (BGM0504) in healthy volunteers: A dose-escalation Phase I study
Molecular dynamics-guided optimization of BGM0504 enhances dual-target agonism for combating diabetes and obesity

compounds.key_findings: BGM0504 is a GLP-1/GIP dual agonist developed by BrightGene Bio-Medical. Phase 1 study (40 volunteers) showed dose-dependent weight loss: -8.30% at 15mg, -7.09% at 10mg, -6.26% at 5mg, -3.24% at 2.5mg. Half-life 4 days supports once-weekly dosing. Preclinical data shows 2-3x more potent than tirzepatide at GLP-1R/GIPR (EC50: 0.031 nM vs 0.086 nM). 55% fibrosis reduction in NASH model. Safety: GI events most common (vomiting, nausea, decreased appetite), 1 case report of cholestatic hepatitis (PMC11557366, recovered with treatment).
compounds.notes: Data from 2 PubMed papers (2024-2025), Phase 1 completed, Phase 2 ongoing. Potency 2-3x higher than tirzepatide.

clinical_trials.results: SUPERIOR to oral semaglutide for HbA1c

compounds.key_findings: Orforglipron (LY3502970) is an oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist from Eli Lilly. Phase 3 program completed with 4 major trials: ACHIEVE-1 (T2D, -7.6% weight loss, HbA1c -1.48%), ATTAIN-1 (Obesity, -11.2% weight loss, 54.6% achieved ≥10%), ATTAIN-2 (Obesity+T2D, -9.6% weight loss), ACHIEVE-3 (SUPERIOR to oral semaglutide for HbA1c). Bioavailability 79.1%. 2 NEJM + 2 Lancet papers (2025-2026). NDA expected 2026, FDA approval expected 2027. GI events 58-59%, discontinuation 9-10%.
compounds.notes: Phase 3 complete, NDA 2026. Superior to oral semaglutide. First oral non-peptide GLP-1 with efficacy matching injectables.

chemical_identifiers.notes: GLP-1/GIP dual receptor agonist, oral small-molecule

compounds.key_findings: Naperiglipron (LY3549492) is an oral small-molecule GLP-1/GIP dual receptor agonist from Eli Lilly. Full chemical structure disclosed via PubChem (CID 155433819, MW 580.6 g/mol). Patents US11655242 and WO2020263695 filed. Clinical trial details not publicly accessible due to technical limitations. No PubMed publications yet (compound too new). Same mechanism as tirzepatide (dual GLP-1/GIP agonist).
compounds.notes: Chemical structure fully characterized. Clinical data limited due to access restrictions. Early-stage Eli Lilly asset.

chemical_identifiers.notes: No direct data found. Related compound THDBH120 (GIPR/GLP-1R dual agonist) has 2026 publication (PMID: 41690462).

compounds.notes: THDBH110 not found in public databases. THDBH120 (related dual agonist) has 2026 publication.

compounds.notes: IND approved 2023-08-14, Phase I ongoing. No PubMed publications yet. Chemical structure not disclosed.

compounds.company: 百极优棠 (广东) 医药科技有限公司 (BPYT / Baiji Youtang)

chemical_identifiers.notes: First-in-class oral small molecule GLP-1 secretagogue (not receptor agonist)

compounds.key_findings: BPYT-01 is a first-in-class oral small molecule GLP-1 secretagogue developed by 百极优棠 (BPYT) with CAS institutes. Unlike GLP-1 receptor agonists, BPYT-01 selectively distributes to intestine and promotes endogenous GLP-1 secretion from L-cells. Discovered from medicinal plant research. China IND approved for T2D (2023-07-18) and Obesity (2024-05-21). Phase I trials actively recruiting. Novel mechanism distinguishes from receptor agonists.
compounds.notes: IND approved for 2 indications (T2D + Obesity). Phase I recruiting. Novel GLP-1 secretagogue mechanism. 5 patents held but numbers not public.

clinical_trials.trial_site: Anhui Medical University Second Hospital, Hefei

compounds.key_findings: RGT-274-CR Capsule is a first-in-class chemical drug from Shanghai Qilu Regor. CDE approved on 2025-05-25 (CXHL2500266) for weight management in adult obesity/overweight. Phase I ongoing as of July 2025 at Anhui Medical University Second Hospital. Company founded 2018, Qilu Pharmaceutical is Series A investor, currently Series B. Related asset RGT-075 (Phase 2 oral GLP-1RA) has Eli Lilly partnership ($50M upfront + $1.5B milestones).
compounds.notes: CDE approved 2025-05-25. Phase I ongoing. Qilu Regor with Eli Lilly partnership on related asset.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Early-stage compound, Roche acquired Carmot for ~$3.1B (2024)

compounds.key_findings: RO7795081/CT-996 is an early-stage compound from Carmot Therapeutics (acquired by Roche in 2024 for ~$3.1B). Not indexed in PubChem or ClinicalTrials.gov as of search date, suggesting preclinical or early Phase I development. CT-996 follows Carmot naming convention (CT-XXX). No publications specifically mention RO7795081 or CT-996. Related compound CT-388 (dual GLP-1/GIP agonist) published in Molecular Metabolism 2026.
compounds.notes: Early-stage Roche/Carmot asset. No public database entries yet. Flag for future monitoring.

chemical_identifiers.notes: First oral GLP-1 receptor agonist, 94% homology to human GLP-1, SNAC absorption enhancer

pubmed_papers.key_2025: 3 major NEJM/Circulation papers on CV outcomes

compounds.key_findings: First oral GLP-1 RA approved by FDA on 2019-09-20. 742+ PubMed publications. HbA1c reduction 0.6-1.4% (dose-dependent). Weight loss up to 15.1% at 50mg (OASIS 1). CV safety established (SOUL 2025). Dosing: 3mg→7mg→14mg once daily on empty stomach. Side effects: nausea, diarrhea, hypoglycemia (>10%); retinopathy monitoring required.
compounds.notes: APPROVED drug with extensive clinical evidence. EMA approved 2020-04-03. China NMPA approved 2020-2021.

compounds.company: Eccogene (诚益生物) → AstraZeneca (阿斯利康)

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, first-in-class

compounds.key_findings: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 RA from Eccogene, licensed to AstraZeneca ($1.825B deal). Phase I completed with 52 participants. Potent GLP-1R binding (IC50 = 2.4 nM). Well tolerated with no SAEs. KEY ADVANTAGE: Once-daily dosing WITHOUT fasting requirements (unlike oral semaglutide). Dose-dependent glucose and weight reductions demonstrated. 9-month NHP study showed NOAEL at 50 mg/kg/day.
compounds.notes: Phase I published 2025. Major differentiator: no fasting required vs Rybelsus.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Long-acting GLP-1 analog, once-weekly subcutaneous

compounds.key_findings: Long-acting GLP-1 analog from Gan & Lee. Once-weekly subcutaneous injection (1-50 μg/kg). Half-life ~7 days in humans. Phase 1 (2025): body weight reduction -1.25 to -1.86 kg (-1.88% to -3.11%) by Day 15. Good tolerability with common AEs being decreased appetite and nausea (mild). No ethnic PK differences between American and Chinese subjects.
compounds.notes: Phase 1 published 2025. Once-weekly dosing. -3.11% weight loss by Day 15.
